Tests that the "NaN" property is not enumerable in the Number object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that iterates over the properties of the `Number` object in JavaScript. It checks if any property name is equal to the string `"NaN"`. If such a property is found, the script calls the `assert` function with `false`, which typically indicates a failed assertion in testing contexts. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on verifying the absence of the `"NaN"` property in the `Number` object.
